items.title: Scheduled Research Cycle Report Published in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The latest research cycle report for May 8, 2026, has been published, reviewing 17 candidates. Key decisions include holding the TROLL memecoin due to its strong liquidity and established history, while seven candidates were skipped due to various red flags. Data source health remains stable, although some Grok probes experienced timeouts.

items.title: Fix empty image_urls handling for generate requests in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the validation for `image_urls` in generate requests, allowing empty arrays to be accepted. Previously, any empty array would trigger a 400 error, impacting integrations like Dify. The fix ensures that empty arrays are treated as valid input for generate actions while maintaining strict checks for edit actions.

items.title: New Research Cycle Completed in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: A new research cycle has been successfully executed, resulting in the publication of a comprehensive report detailing data source health and intent decisions. The report includes diagnostics, explicit skip intents, and a thorough analysis of liquidity issues. Key findings highlight the status of various candidates and their associated risks.

items.title: Standardize PVC Naming for Studio in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update renames the studio-side Persistent Volume Claim from `caddy` to `caddy-studio`, aligning it with the naming convention used for other resources. Key changes include modifications to the `studio-proxy.yaml` file to inline the PVC declaration and update deployment references. This ensures consistency across both studio and platform resources, enhancing clarity and maintainability.

items.title: Backfill missing i18n keys and switch to Python-based translation in Frontend Web 📝
items.summary: Replaces the old transmart workflow with new Python scripts for translating and verifying i18n keys. Adds a coverage guard to ensure all locales match zh-CN, and backfills 262 missing keys in fr and ko survey.json files. Removes transmart dependencies, cached sentinel files, and updates CI/translation workflows for Python 3.12. The new process is idempotent, strict on missing translations, and prevents silent regressions.

items.title: Streamline i18n coverage and backfill missing keys in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This PR replaces `@transmart/cli` with a Python-based translation script and introduces a coverage guard to prevent missing keys. It backfills 1056 keys across 16 locales and 37 namespaces, ensuring full alignment with the zh-CN source. CI workflows now validate key coverage during PRs and after auto-translation. All previous cache files and transmart dependencies have been removed for a cleaner setup.

items.title: Streamline Caddy proxy naming for clarity in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This PR renames the platform-side Caddy reverse proxy from `caddy-platform-tenant-proxy` to `caddy-platform-proxy`, aligning it with Nexior's parallel rename. All Kubernetes resource names, YAML files, and related references are updated, while DNS and PVCs remain untouched to ensure backwards compatibility. The change improves clarity and symmetry between the platform and studio proxies, simplifying internal operations and deployments.

items.title: Rename tenant proxy to studio proxy for clarity in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This PR renames internal Kubernetes resources from `caddy-tenant-proxy` to `caddy-studio-proxy` to more accurately reflect that it fronts only studio sub-sites. The deployment YAML, ConfigMap, Service, and related labels were updated, while DNS and the persistent volume remain unchanged. Minor documentation in `site_domain.ts` and the deploy script were also updated to match the new naming. No production tenants are affected, so HTTPS certificates will refresh on first access without disruption.

items.title: Replace Transmart with safe diff-and-fill translator in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This PR removes `@transmart/cli` and replaces it with a Python-based diff-and-fill translator for i18n. Missing keys across all locales are now detected and translated with strict validation, ensuring no dropped translations. The workflow has been updated to use Python 3.12, and idempotent runs produce zero unnecessary writes. Cache files and unsafe Node dependencies have been fully removed, improving reliability and coverage.

items.title: Completed SolRadar 2026-05-08 research cycle in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The scheduled SolRadar research report for 2026-05-08 was published as a single-file addition. It includes fully populated research content with explicit actions, reasons, and reviewed candidates. Data source health is documented with provider degradation details, and narrative gating rules were applied to flag suspicious candidates. Key assets like SOL were assessed for liquidity and risk, while high-risk pump-and-bump candidates were skipped.
